overview: This deck aims to generate an overwhelming army of 1/1 Human tokens by repeatedly casting spells each turn, leveraging Ashling, the Limitless and Risen Reef as core engines. It wins by out-resourcing opponents through spell density and token multiplication, overwhelming both aggressive and control strategies with unstoppable board presence.
win_conditions: Ashling, the Limitless (entering triggers + second spell triggers), Risen Reef, and other token generators.
Win by casting multiple spells in a single turn to flood the board, then attacking with the token army.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing untapped or fetchable basics), at least one mana rock (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, etc.), and a 1-2 mana cantrip or creature.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan any hand with zero lands, only high-cost spells, or no early interaction/spell generation tools.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring or a cheap mana artifact.
If possible, cast a 1-mana cantrip or creature to start the 'second spell' trigger.
Turn 2: Ramp into dual lands or fetch basics, cast a second spell to trigger Ashling/Risen Reef, or play Ashling directly if mana allows.
Prioritize untapped land drops to enable multi-casting.
mid_game: avoid overcommitting to one big spell if you can cast two smaller ones to trigger token generation.
Manage hand size by cycling through cantrips.
Use the graveyard strategically but avoid relying on it since the deck is spell-centric.
Commit threats when you have untapped mana for at least 3 spells in a turn.
Hold back if opponents have sweepers or if you're low on resources.
Prioritize protecting Ashling and Risen Reef with counterspells or protection spells.
late_game: Ashling, the Limitless (entering triggers + second spell triggers), Risen Reef, and other token generators.
Win by casting multiple spells in a single turn to flood the board, then attacking with the token army.
Go all-in when you have untapped mana for at least 3-4 spells in a turn, or when opponents have no sweepers in hand.
Commit when you can protect your board or have a backup plan if wiped.

sequencing_tips: Remember that Ashling's second-spell trigger only counts spells you cast, not tokens or triggered abilities.
Risen Reef triggers on every spell you cast, so stacking cheap cantrips is key.
Avoid overcommitting to one big spell
Watch for 'summoning sickness' on your tokens—they can attack immediately if they enter on your turn, but be mindful of combat math.
In Commander, always be aware of the 'conspiracy' rule regarding sideboards
if playing in a sanctioned environment, use a sideboard from your command zone or adjust your main deck pre-game accordingly.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, redundant spells to force counterspells. Employ mana rocks to cast around counters. Use instant-speed spells to protect your key creatures.
Apply early pressure with tokens. Use removal on their mana sources. Don't let them stabilize; keep casting spells to overwhelm their ramp strategy.
Hold up removal/wipes for turn 3-4. Use your early tokens to trade efficiently. Stabilize with board wipes, then transition to your own win condition.
Out-grind them with card advantage and multi-casting. Use cantrips to dig for answers. Attack with tokens to force them to spend resources on removal instead of their win condition.

Win by having better card advantage, more efficient mana, or additional protection for your key creatures. Prioritize removing their Ashling/Risen Reef and controlling the board with targeted removal.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), targeted removal on your key creatures, and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells, as you can capitalize on their vulnerability.
